mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of low-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.4pp threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.04) when tested against historical demand cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and economic momentum, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive equity returns over the next month during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with a negative Rank IC or a win rate below 50% in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly payroll employment growth reflects a resilient labor market and sustained economic activity, providing a stable foundation for corporate earnings and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in over 55% of positive-growth months across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 230 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDP growth outpaces industrial production signals supply chain constraints and inventory accumulation, pressuring corporate margins and generating negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts positive returns in over 65% of cases, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in retail sales confirm weakening consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following two consecutive retail sales declines, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below chance in regime-specific samples.
